violations.description: Time as a public health control. — --Food was not marked or identified with the time within which it must be cooked, served, or discarded. A five gallon bucket of raw home fries in the kitchen temped at 61.5 degrees. This was not marked with a time in which it must used, or returned to refrigeration. This can not exceed 41 degrees, or must be used within four hours.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. The outside of the butter spread container by the griddle needs to be cleaned. The bottom of the prep cooler in the kitchen needs to be cleaned.--Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. The doors, gaskets, and handles on the reach in freezer by the fryers needs to be cleaned. The counter under the grill needs to be cleaned.--Ventilation hood system was not cleaned properly and/or as frequently as required. The hood system and filters need to be cleaned.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. The floor under the grill needs to be cleaned.--Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not maintained in good repair. The basement floor by the ice machine is cracked, broken, and no longer a cleanable surface.

Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --In-use utensil was not properly stored during pauses in food preparation or dispensing. Handle of the ice scoop was stored in the ice in the ice bin by the hand sink.

Time as a public health control. — --Food was not marked or identified with the time within which it must be cooked, served, or discarded. A container of cut celery on the counter by the prep cooler was not marked with a time it was prepared or must be discarded.

General equipment and utensils - Maintenance and operation. — --Nonfood contact equipment/utensil was not maintained in a good repair or sound condition. The counter at the three compartment sink had broken and missing laminate with exposed wood.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. The gaskets on the stainless steel freezer in the basement and the handles on the white fridges and freezers need to be cleaned.

Plumbing system. — --Plumbing system was not designed, constructed, operated and/or maintained according to regulation. The faucet at the kitchen hand sink was not working properly. With valves on full very little water was coming out of the faucet.

Design and construction of floors, walls, and ceilings. — --Floors, walls, and/or ceiling were not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent, and easily cleanable. The basement floor is pitted and cracked. This has food storage and single use storage. This floor is no longer a cleanable surface.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. The floors behind the cooking equipment need to be cleaned. The basement floor needs to be cleaned.

Food display and service. — --Potentially hazardous food was not kept at an internal temperature of 41F or below during display or service. Cut celery on the counter by the prep cooler temped at 59.3 degrees. This must be kept below 41 degrees at all times, or marked when it must be discarded.
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pt deducted)
Designed, constructed, installed, and maintained (1 pt deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (1 pt deducted)
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pt deducted)
Proper storage, labeling, use (3 pts deducted)
Clean, proper use, storage, sanitizing solution (2 pts deducted)
Hot holding and cold holding temperatures maintained, unwrapped food not reserved (5 pts deducted)
Designed, constructed, installed, and maintained (1 pt deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (1 pt deducted)
Properly designed, located, constructed, operated, maintained, cleaned and chemical test kit provided (2 pts deducted)
